data.content:
Employer
The Employer is a legal entity or individual that enters into formal labor contracts with workers, thereby establishing legally governed employment relationships and serving as one of the two principal contracting parties in labor law. As such, the Employer holds managerial authority—including the right to direct work activities, issue instructions, set labor quotas, determine piece-rate remuneration standards (within statutory working hour limits), and terminate contracts under legally defined conditions—provided terminations are accompanied by written notice and justified under applicable labor law. When contracts are rendered invalid due to the Employer’s fault, it bears liability for resulting damages.

The Employer carries extensive statutory obligations across multiple domains. It must pay wages in full and on time, ensuring compensation meets or exceeds the local minimum wage, and determine wage levels and distribution methods in alignment with operational characteristics and economic performance. It is required to compensate employees during statutory leave periods—including holidays, sick leave, maternity leave, and other socially protected absences—and to comply with protections specific to vulnerable groups, such as female and underage workers. The Employer must ensure workers receive at least one day of rest per week, formulate and enforce labor regulations governing working hours and wage payments, and consult with the labor union and affected workers before implementing extended working hours necessitated by operational needs.

Occupational safety and health constitute core responsibilities: the Employer must establish and maintain robust safety and health systems, enforce national regulations and technical standards, supply appropriate protective equipment, provide mandatory safety education, and ensure safe working conditions; failure may trigger legal accountability, including penalties under provisions such as Article 96 for safety violations. Additionally, the Employer is obligated to participate in the national social insurance system and pay required premiums on behalf of employees for pensions, medical care, unemployment, work-related injury, and maternity insurance. It must also engage in equal negotiations with workers—often through representative bodies—to safeguard their lawful rights and interests, improve collective welfare, enhance workers’ welfare benefits, and may establish or engage with labor dispute mediation committees.

Beyond these affirmative duties, the Employer is subject to inspection and regulation by labor administrative departments regarding compliance with labor laws and regulations. It is legally obligated to report and handle workplace accidents and occupational diseases, and to fulfill all contractual terms—including timely payment and provision of adequate working conditions. The Employer bears responsibility for safeguarding commercial secrets and may suffer economic losses due to breaches of contract or confidentiality by workers. Conversely, the Employer itself may violate labor law—for example, by unlawfully terminating employment contracts or deliberately delaying contract signing—thereby incurring legal liability and remedial obligations. Collectively, these roles position the Employer as a central, yet strictly regulated, actor in labor relations—exercising authority only within boundaries defined by law, collective bargaining, and enforceable worker protections.<SEP>An employer is a legal entity or individual that hires workers and is prohibited from employing minors under the age of sixteen, except under specific conditions.<SEP>The employer is the entity that may carry out staff reductions under specific legal conditions, such as imminent bankruptcy or severe operational difficulties.<SEP>An employer is an entity that hires workers and is subject to labor regulations, including prohibitions on hiring minors under sixteen.<SEP>An employer is an entity that hires laborers and is legally restricted from terminating employment contracts under certain protected conditions.<SEP>The Employer is an entity that may obstruct labor administration departments and retaliate against whistleblowers, subject to penalties under labor law.

Piece-Rate Worker
A piece-rate worker is a laborer whose compensation is determined based on the quantity of work completed, subject to statutory working hour and remuneration standards.

Article 36
Article 36 specifies the statutory working hour system in Chinese labor law, which serves as the basis for determining labor quotas and remuneration under Article 37.<SEP>Article 36 is a specific provision in Chinese labor law that establishes the standard working hours system.<SEP>Article 36 is a statutory provision governing standard working hours and rest periods, which certain enterprises may be exempted from applying.

Article 37
Article 37 is a provision in Chinese labor law addressing wage and working hour standards for piece-rate workers.

data.entity_name: Labor Law of the People's Republic of China
data.content:
Labor Law of the People's Republic of China
The Labor Law of the People's Republic of China is the overarching legal framework containing Articles 36 and 37, governing employment conditions, working hours, and remuneration standards.<SEP>The Labor Law of the People's Republic of China is a legal framework established to protect workers' rights, regulate labor relations, and support socialist market economy development.<SEP>The Labor Law of the People's Republic of China is the foundational legal framework governing employment relationships, within which Article 94 is situated.

Article Seventy-Four
Article Seventy-Four is a legal provision stipulating the responsibilities of social insurance fund administration and supervisory agencies, and prohibiting the misappropriation of social insurance funds.

Social Insurance Fund Administration Agency
The Social Insurance Fund Administration Agency is responsible for the collection, management, and operation of social insurance funds in accordance with the law, and bears the responsibility for preserving and increasing the value of these funds.

Social Insurance Fund Supervisory Agency
The Social Insurance Fund Supervisory Agency is responsible for supervising the collection, management, and operation of social insurance funds in accordance with the law.

Social Insurance Fund
The Social Insurance Fund is a legally mandated fund used to provide social insurance benefits, subject to strict rules on collection, management, operation, supervision, and protection against misuse.<SEP>The Social Insurance Fund is a financial mechanism established by the State to support the provision of social insurance benefits.<SEP>The Social Insurance Fund is a financial resource established to provide social security benefits, including pensions, medical care, unemployment, work injury, and maternity insurance.<SEP>The Social Insurance Fund is a financial mechanism whose funding sources are determined by insurance type and which is gradually implementing social pooling.

Any Organization or Individual
The law explicitly prohibits any organization or individual from misappropriating the Social Insurance Fund, ensuring its dedicated use and legal protection.

Special Operations Qualification
Special operations qualification is a formal certification granted upon successful completion of specialized training, authorizing individuals to perform designated special operations.

Specialized Training
Specialized training is a mandatory educational process required for special operations workers to acquire necessary skills and safety knowledge.

Article 55
Article 55 is a regulatory provision stipulating that workers engaged in special operations must undergo specialized training and obtain corresponding qualifications.

Special Operations Workers
Special operations workers are laborers who must undergo specialized training and obtain certification to perform hazardous or specialized tasks.

Probation Period
The probation period is a trial phase in a labor contract during which the employer may assess whether the laborer meets the employment requirements.<SEP>The probation period is a trial phase at the beginning of employment during which either party may terminate the labor contract more freely.

Gross Negligence
Gross negligence is a serious failure in performing duties, resulting in significant harm to the employer's interests.

Labor Discipline
Labor discipline refers to the behavioral standards and rules established by the employer that laborers are required to follow.<SEP>Labor discipline is a mandatory clause outlining expected conduct, rules, and behavioral standards in the workplace.

Laborer
The Laborer is an individual who enters into a labor contract with an employer and is governed by labor law in multiple interrelated dimensions. As a party to such a contract, the Laborer is subject to statutory provisions concerning contract termination—including obligations to provide written notice when initiating termination—and is bound by ancillary legal duties such as confidentiality. The Laborer performs work under this contractual relationship and is entitled to receive wages, including payment during statutory holidays, marriage leave, bereavement leave, and lawful participation in social activities. Beyond remuneration, the Laborer is guaranteed access to social insurance benefits under legally defined circumstances, reflecting the protective framework of labor legislation.

The Laborer also holds substantive rights aimed at safeguarding dignity, equity, and voice in the workplace. These include protection from discrimination under Article 12 of applicable labor law and the right to participate democratically in enterprise management or engage in equal negotiations with employers concerning the protection of lawful rights and interests. Collectively, these rights and obligations position the Laborer not merely as a service provider but as a legally recognized subject with enforceable entitlements and procedural safeguards embedded in national labor regulation.<SEP>The laborer is an individual who has the right to terminate the labor contract under specific circumstances outlined in Article 32.<SEP>A laborer is a worker who has the legal right to join and form trade unions.<SEP>A laborer is an individual who works for an employer and is protected under labor law from unjust termination in specific circumstances.

Embezzlement and Fraud
Embezzlement and fraud refer to intentional unlawful acts by a laborer for personal gain that damage the employer's interests.

Labor Contract
Labor Contract is a legally binding agreement that formally establishes and governs the employment relationship between an employer and an employee—or, equivalently, a worker and an employer. As a foundational legal instrument, it defines the mutual rights, obligations, and responsibilities of both parties throughout the duration of employment and is explicitly regulated by national or regional labor law. The contract must be entered into voluntarily and, in many jurisdictions, is required to be in written form and include specific mandatory clauses—such as compensation, working hours, job duties, workplace safety provisions, and conditions for termination—to ensure enforceability and statutory worker protection.

Core structural elements commonly stipulated in a Labor Contract include provisions for a probationary period, during which either party may assess suitability under mutually agreed terms; detailed grounds, procedures, and potential severance entitlements associated with termination; and mechanisms for resolving disputes in alignment with labor standards and regulatory frameworks. Its legal significance extends beyond defining work expectations: it serves as a primary tool for enforcing statutory protections, upholding labor rights, and adjudicating liabilities—including those arising under specific legal provisions, such as Article 99, where termination status directly determines employer liability. Across all descriptions, the Labor Contract is consistently characterized as a formal, legally enforceable instrument subject to compliance with applicable labor legislation and integral to maintaining equitable and lawful employment relations.<SEP>The labor contract is a legally binding agreement between the laborer and employer, defining rights, obligations, and conditions of employment.<SEP>A labor contract is a legal agreement between an employer and employee, governed by principles of equality, voluntariness, and mutual consultation.

Employer's Rules and Regulations
Employer's rules and regulations are formal internal policies that govern employee conduct and operational procedures.

Criminal Liability
Criminal liability arises when a laborer is formally prosecuted and held legally accountable for committing a crime.<SEP>Criminal Liability is the legal consequence imposed on responsible personnel when their actions constitute a crime under Article 96.<SEP>Criminal Liability refers to the legal responsibility imposed on individuals who commit crimes, including penalties such as imprisonment or fines.<SEP>Criminal Liability refers to legal responsibility imposed when staff members commit crimes such as abuse of power, dereliction of duty, or favoritism.<SEP>Criminal liability refers to legal responsibility under the Criminal Law for serious negligence leading to major accidents.<SEP>Criminal Liability refers to the legal responsibility for criminal acts, which may be imposed on responsible personnel if obstruction or retaliation constitutes a crime under Article 101.

Article Twenty-Five
Article Twenty-Five is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying circumstances under which an employer may lawfully terminate a labor contract.

Article 23
Article 23 is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying the conditions under which a labor contract terminates automatically.

Chinese Labor Law
Chinese Labor Law is the statutory legal framework governing employment relationships in China, within which Article 23 is situated.<SEP>Chinese Labor Law is the legal framework governing labor relations and establishing special protections for female workers and minor workers.

Contract Termination
Contract termination refers to the cessation of a labor contract upon fulfillment of statutory or agreed conditions, such as expiration of term or occurrence of stipulated termination events.<SEP>Contract termination is the legal process by which a labor contract is ended, permitted here upon mutual agreement of the parties.

Collective Contract
A collective contract is a legally binding agreement signed in accordance with the law that applies to both the enterprise and all its employees.<SEP>A Collective Contract is a legally binding agreement covering labor remuneration, working hours, rest and vacation, occupational safety and health, insurance, and welfare.<SEP>A collective contract is an agreement negotiated between employers and a labor union representing employees, covering working conditions and rights.<SEP>Collective contract is an agreement negotiated between employers and worker representatives (e.g., unions) covering terms and conditions for a group of workers, as referenced in the chapter title.

Individual Labor Contract
An individual labor contract is an agreement between an employee and an enterprise specifying labor conditions and remuneration, required to meet minimum standards established by the collective contract.

Article 35
Article 35 is a legal provision stipulating that legally signed collective contracts bind both the enterprise and all its employees, and that individual labor contracts must meet or exceed the labor conditions and remuneration standards set in the collective contract.

Enterprise
An enterprise is a business entity bound by the collective contract and responsible for complying with its provisions regarding labor conditions and remuneration.<SEP>The Enterprise is the employer entity that engages in collective contract negotiations with its employees.<SEP>An enterprise is a business entity that may apply for alternative work and rest arrangements due to its production characteristics.

Employee
The Employee is a worker who enters into a labor contract with an employer, forming the individual party to that agreement and assuming obligations to perform work duties as specified in the contract. As such, the Employee is bound by contractual terms including confidentiality obligations concerning the employer’s commercial secrets. The Employee’s rights and protections are grounded in both individual labor contracts and broader regulatory frameworks, particularly where collective contracts apply: in such cases, the individual labor contract must meet or exceed the standards established collectively. The Employee is entitled to economic compensation under specified legal conditions, including termination of the contract—whether lawfully initiated by the employer (e.g., due to inability to perform work after medical leave or failure to meet job requirements) or unlawfully executed (e.g., through unjustified dismissal or delayed contract signing). Additionally, the Employee is legally guaranteed labor safety and hygiene conditions, access to appropriate protective equipment, and mandatory regular health checks—especially when engaged in hazardous occupations. Should the employment contract be deemed invalid, the Employee is recognized as the party suffering damage and is therefore entitled to compensation. Thus, the Employee occupies a dual role: as a duty-bound contractual party obligated to fulfill agreed-upon responsibilities, and as a legally protected individual vested with enforceable rights across dimensions of remuneration, health and safety, procedural fairness, and redress for contractual violations.

Worker
The Worker is an individual who enters into a labor relationship with an employer through a formal labor contract, thereby establishing legally recognized employment status. As such, the Worker is entitled to core statutory protections and benefits under labor law, including receipt of wages no lower than the locally prescribed minimum wage standard, eligibility for overtime pay in accordance with regulatory stipulations, and guaranteed access to statutory holidays. The Worker is also required to participate in the national social insurance system, entailing both entitlement to coverage and the obligation to pay applicable social insurance premiums.

In carrying out assigned duties, the Worker must strictly abide by safety operating procedures during the work process, reflecting a shared responsibility for occupational health and safety. While the Worker operates under the employer’s direction—including adherence to work orders—the legal framework imposes health safeguards when working hours are extended under specific conditions, acknowledging potential risks associated with prolonged labor. Notably, one description indicates the Worker may be subject to potentially unsafe work orders; this introduces tension with the requirement to follow safety procedures and suggests an area of regulatory vulnerability or contested interpretation—where legal protections may exist in principle but face practical enforcement challenges. Collectively, these descriptions portray the Worker as a rights-bearing yet structurally subordinate party within a regulated labor relationship, defined by contractual formality, statutory entitlements, procedural obligations, and embedded power asymmetries.<SEP>A worker is an individual employed under a labor contract and whose actions may trigger legal liabilities for employers.<SEP>A worker is an individual employed by an employer and protected under labor law provisions on working time.<SEP>The Worker is a party in labor disputes and is entitled to rest, recuperation, convalescence, and protection of lawful rights and interests during dispute resolution.<SEP>The worker is the individual employed by the employer and entitled to at least one day of rest per week.<SEP>A person engaged in labor who is protected under the reporting and handling system for workplace accidents and occupational diseases.<SEP>The worker is an individual employed by an employer and entitled to labor safety protections, whose life and property may be harmed due to safety failures.

Rest Day Work
Rest Day Work refers to work scheduled on designated rest days without compensatory time off, requiring double wage payment.

Article 44
Article 44 is a provision in China's Labor Law specifying mandatory overtime wage standards for employers.

Statutory Holiday Work
Statutory Holiday Work refers to work performed on officially recognized public holidays, requiring triple wage payment.

Overtime Work
Overtime Work refers to work performed beyond normal working hours, triggering higher wage compensation requirements.<SEP>Overtime work is prohibited for pregnant women beyond seven months and for women during lactation.

China's Labor Law
China's Labor Law is the overarching legal framework containing Article 44 and governing labor relations, working hours, and wage standards in China.<SEP>China's Labor Law is the statutory legal framework governing labor relations, including working hours, rest periods, and protections for workers.<SEP>China's Labor Law is the statutory framework containing Article 99, which governs employer liability in cases of hiring workers still under existing labor contracts.

Trade Union
The Trade Union represents employees in signing collective contracts with enterprises where it is established.<SEP>The Trade Union participates in labor dispute mediation through representation on the Labor Dispute Mediation Committee and appoints its chairperson.<SEP>The trade union is a representative body that must be consulted by the employer prior to staff reductions, as stipulated in the regulation.<SEP>A trade union represents and safeguards the legitimate rights and interests of laborers and conducts activities independently in accordance with the law.

Enterprise Employee Party
The Enterprise Employee Party refers to the collective body of employees in an enterprise who negotiate labor-related matters with the enterprise.

Employee Representative Congress
The Employee Representative Congress is a deliberative body through which draft collective contracts are discussed and approved.

Labor Remuneration
Labor Remuneration refers to the wages and salaries paid to employees, one of the key subjects covered in collective contracts.<SEP>Labor remuneration is a mandatory clause specifying the wages, salary structure, and payment terms for the employee.<SEP>Labor remuneration denotes the wages or compensation owed to the laborer as stipulated in the labor contract.

Employee-Designated Representative
An Employee-Designated Representative is a person elected by employees to sign collective contracts on their behalf in enterprises without a trade union.

Working Hours
Working Hours denotes the scheduled time during which employees are required to work, regulated under collective contracts.<SEP>Working Hours refers to the legally defined or contractually agreed period during which employees are required to perform work.<SEP>Working hours refer to the time during which a worker is required to perform duties for an employer, regulated by the Labor Law.<SEP>Working hours refer to the legally defined duration of employment, and unlawful extension triggers administrative penalties.

Rest And Vacation
Rest And Vacation encompasses statutory rest periods and paid leave entitlements for employees, as stipulated in collective contracts.

Occupational Safety And Health
Occupational Safety And Health covers measures and standards to ensure safe and healthy working conditions, addressed in collective contracts.

Insurance And Welfare
Insurance And Welfare includes social insurance contributions and supplementary welfare benefits negotiated between employees and enterprises.

Social Insurance
Social insurance is a state-developed system that provides assistance and compensation to workers in cases of old age, illness, work-related injury, unemployment, and childbirth.<SEP>Social insurance is a public welfare system improved by the State to provide financial protection to workers in cases such as unemployment, illness, or retirement.

Vocational Skill Standards
Vocational skill standards are official benchmarks established by the State for specified occupations to guide training and assessment.

State
The State refers to the national government of China, which serves as the central authority responsible for comprehensive labor governance, social welfare development, and economic policy implementation. It enacts and enforces labor protection laws—particularly those safeguarding female and minor workers—and establishes binding labor standards across sectors. In the domain of employment, the State actively promotes job creation by fostering economic and social development, supporting individual business operations, and expanding employment opportunities through targeted initiatives. To enhance workforce capability and adaptability, it develops vocational training programs and vocational education systems, while also determining occupational classifications to standardize labor market structures.

The State plays a pivotal role in social security architecture, establishing and improving social insurance systems—including pension, medical, unemployment, work-related injury, and maternity insurance—for both employers and workers. It encourages and promotes insurance-related policies to broaden coverage and strengthen risk protection. Complementing this, the State develops social welfare undertakings and constructs public welfare facilities—such as rest homes, sanatoriums, and convalescent centers—to support workers’ rest, recuperation, and overall well-being. Furthermore, it regulates income distribution to advance fairness and coordinates labor relations to maintain industrial harmony. Collectively, these functions reflect the State’s integrated mandate to ensure labor rights, foster inclusive growth, and sustain long-term socioeconomic stability in China.<SEP>The State refers to the national government of China, which establishes systems for reporting and handling workplace accidents and occupational diseases.

Vocational Training
Vocational training is a state-supported initiative aimed at developing workers' occupational skills, improving worker quality, and enhancing employment and job capabilities.<SEP>Vocational Training refers to education and instruction focused on developing specific skills for employment.

People's Governments at All Levels
People's Governments at All Levels are local administrative bodies required to integrate vocational training development into socioeconomic planning and encourage diverse stakeholders to conduct such training.

Occupational Classification
Occupational classification is a system determined by the State to categorize professions and underpin the development of occupational skill standards and certification.

Professional Qualification Certificate System
The professional qualification certificate system is a State-mandated framework requiring certification for certain occupations, administered by registered assessment institutions.

Assessment and Appraisal Institutions
Assessment and appraisal institutions are entities registered with the State to administer vocational skill assessments and certifications for workers.

Employers
Employers are obligated to establish vocational training systems, allocate and use vocational training funds as prescribed, and conduct planned training for workers, especially those in technical trades who must be trained before上岗(taking up their posts).<SEP>Employers are entities obligated to comply with labor protection regulations, including assigning appropriate work and conducting health checks for minor workers.<SEP>Employers are entities responsible for labor management and are encouraged by the State to establish supplementary insurance for their workers.<SEP>Employers include enterprises and individual economic organizations within the People's Republic of China, as well as state organs, public institutions, and social organizations that establish labor contract relationships with workers.

Workers
Workers are individuals whose occupational skills, quality, employment capacity, and work capacity are enhanced through vocational training, and who are entitled to social insurance benefits under specified life circumstances.<SEP>Workers are individuals employed under labor contracts and are entitled to paid annual leave after meeting the continuous employment requirement.<SEP>Workers are individuals subject to the Labor Hour System, whose daily and weekly working hours are regulated.<SEP>Workers are the individuals who participate in social voluntary labor, labor competitions, rationalization proposals, scientific research, technological innovation, and invention activities.<SEP>Workers are individuals employed by employers and are the beneficiaries of supplementary insurance and encouraged to engage in savings-based insurance.<SEP>Workers are individuals who voluntarily organize for employment or engage in individual business activities to achieve employment.<SEP>Workers are individuals who form labor relationships with employers and possess rights including equal employment, remuneration, rest and vacation, occupational safety and health protection, vocational training, social insurance, and labor dispute resolution.

Technical Workers
Technical workers are a category of workers who, by law, must undergo vocational training before commencing work in their designated technical trades.

Article 50
Article 50 is a provision in China's Labor Law that mandates monthly monetary wage payment to workers and prohibits unauthorized deductions or unjustified delays.

Wage Payment
Wage Payment refers to the legal requirement that wages must be paid in monetary form to workers on a monthly basis.<SEP>Wage payment encompasses timely and full remuneration to workers; withholding or unjustified delay constitutes a violation subject to enforcement.

Labor Law Article 50
Labor Law Article 50 stipulates the rules for wage payment, prohibiting unauthorized deductions or unjustified delays.

National Standards
National standards are official benchmarks established by the state for labor safety and health facilities, requiring compliance in all relevant engineering projects.

New Construction Projects
New construction projects are engineering endeavors that must integrate labor safety and health facilities simultaneously with the main project in design, construction, and commissioning.

Renovation Projects
Renovation projects are engineering endeavors that must integrate labor safety and health facilities simultaneously with the main project in design, construction, and commissioning.

Simultaneous Commissioning
Simultaneous commissioning is a regulatory requirement mandating that labor safety and health facilities be put into production and use concurrently with the main engineering project.

Expansion Projects
Expansion projects are engineering endeavors that must integrate labor safety and health facilities simultaneously with the main project in design, construction, and commissioning.

Simultaneous Construction
Simultaneous construction is a regulatory requirement mandating that labor safety and health facilities be built concurrently with the main engineering project.

Simultaneous Design
Simultaneous design is a regulatory requirement mandating that labor safety and health facilities be planned concurrently with the main engineering project.

Labor Safety and Health Facilities
Labor safety and health facilities refer to infrastructure and equipment designed to ensure worker safety and health, mandated to meet national standards.

Article 53
Article 53 is a legal provision stipulating requirements for labor safety and health facilities in engineering projects.

State Council
The State Council is the highest executive organ of state power in China and receives备案(filing) of minimum wage standards from local governments.<SEP>The State Council is the highest executive organ of state power in China, responsible for formulating the specific implementation measures for the national paid annual leave system.<SEP>The State Council is the highest executive organ of state power in the People's Republic of China and receives备案(filing) of local implementation plans for labor contract systems.

Municipality People's Government
The Municipality People's Government is responsible for setting the specific minimum wage standards within its administrative region.

Autonomous Region People's Government
The Autonomous Region People's Government is responsible for setting the specific minimum wage standards within its administrative region.

Minimum Wage Guarantee System
The Minimum Wage Guarantee System is a national policy ensuring that workers receive wages not below a government-mandated minimum level.

Provincial People's Government
The Provincial People's Government is responsible for setting the specific minimum wage standards within its administrative region.<SEP>The Provincial People's Government is the administrative authority of a province, autonomous region, or municipality directly under the Central Government, responsible for implementing labor contract regulations in accordance with national law and local conditions.

Minimum Wage Standard
The Minimum Wage Standard is a legally defined threshold for wages, set by provincial-level governments and filed with the State Council, which employers must observe.<SEP>The Minimum Wage Standard is a regulatory benchmark for the lowest remuneration employers may legally pay workers, determined and adjusted based on multiple socioeconomic factors.

Article 48
Article 48 is a provision in Chinese labor law that establishes the Minimum Wage Guarantee System and defines related responsibilities and obligations.

Threat
Threat is a prohibited method used to compel labor, as specified in Article 96.

Violence
Violence is one of the coercive means prohibited under Article 96 for forcing labor.

Forced Labor
Forced Labor refers to labor imposed through violence, threats, or illegal restriction of personal freedom, prohibited under Article 96.<SEP>Forced labor refers to work compelled by violence, threats, or unlawful restriction of personal freedom, which entitles the laborer to immediate termination of the contract.

Responsible Personnel
Responsible Personnel are individuals held accountable for unlawful employer conduct and subject to administrative or criminal penalties.<SEP>Responsible Personnel are individuals held legally accountable, including potential criminal liability, for failing to address accident hazards that result in major accidents.

Article 96
Article 96 is a legal provision specifying penalties for employers who use coercive means to force labor.

Public Security Organs
The Public Security Organs are the law enforcement authorities empowered to impose administrative penalties such as detention, fines, or warnings on responsible personnel.

Illegal Restriction of Personal Freedom
Illegal Restriction of Personal Freedom is a prohibited coercive measure for forcing labor under Article 96.

Chapter Four
Chapter Four is titled "Working Hours and Rest and Vacation" and is part of a larger document or regulation.

Rest and Vacation
Rest and Vacation encompasses statutory rest periods, holidays, and leave entitlements granted to employees for recuperation and personal time.

Minor Workers
Minor workers are defined as laborers aged sixteen to under eighteen years old and are subject to special labor protections.

Third-Level Physical Labor Intensity
Third-level physical labor intensity is a classification of strenuous work prohibited for female workers during menstruation, pregnancy, and lactation.

Menstruation
Menstruation is a physiological condition during which female workers must be protected from high-altitude, low-temperature, cold-water, and third-level physical labor intensity work.

Fourth-Level Physical Labor Intensity
Fourth-level physical labor intensity is a classification of strenuous work prohibited for female and minor workers.

Female Workers
Female workers are a protected group under labor law, entitled to special protections during menstruation, pregnancy, and lactation.<SEP>Female workers are a protected group under labor laws, entitled to special protections as outlined in Chapter Seven.

Mine Underground Work
Mine underground work is prohibited for both female workers and minor workers under Chinese labor law.

Pregnancy
Pregnancy is a protected condition during which female workers must not be assigned third-level physical labor intensity work or prohibited tasks, and those over seven months pregnant must not be assigned overtime or night shifts.<SEP>Pregnancy is a protected physiological condition for female laborers, during which termination of employment is prohibited.

Health Check
Health check is a mandatory periodic medical examination required for minor workers by employers.

Night Shift Work
Night shift work is prohibited for pregnant women beyond seven months and for women during lactation.

Ninety-Day Maternity Leave
Ninety-day maternity leave is the minimum statutory leave period granted to female workers upon childbirth.

Lactation
Lactation refers to the period when a female worker is nursing an infant under one year old, during which she must not be assigned third-level physical labor intensity work or prohibited tasks, nor overtime or night shifts.

Toxic and Harmful Work
Toxic and harmful work is prohibited for minor workers under Chinese labor law.

Task-Based Labor Contract
A task-based labor contract is a type of employment agreement that ends upon completion of a specific work assignment, as defined in Article 20 of the Labor Contract Law.

Ten-Year Continuous Employment
Ten-year continuous employment refers to a condition under which an employee working for the same employer for over ten years may be entitled to an open-ended labor contract, per Article 20.

Labor Contract Law
The Labor Contract Law is a legal framework governing employment relationships in China, within which Article 20 is situated.<SEP>The Labor Contract Law is a statutory framework governing labor contracts, including conditions for termination and confidentiality obligations.<SEP>The Labor Contract Law is the legal framework governing employment relationships in China, including provisions on contract termination.

Labor Contract Law Article 20
Article 20 of the Labor Contract Law specifies the types of labor contract durations, including fixed-term, open-ended, and task-based contracts.

Fixed-Term Labor Contract
A fixed-term labor contract is a type of employment agreement with a defined start and end date, as stipulated in Article 20 of the Labor Contract Law.

Open-Ended Labor Contract
An open-ended labor contract is a type of employment agreement without a predetermined termination date, applicable under conditions such as ten years of continuous service with the same employer, as outlined in Article 20.

data.entity_name: Chapter Six Labor Safety and Health
data.content:
Chapter Six Labor Safety and Health
Chapter Six Labor Safety and Health is a section title addressing regulations and practices related to workplace safety and occupational health.

Article Thirty-One
Article Thirty-One is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying the procedure for laborers to terminate employment contracts.

Thirty-Day Notice Period
The thirty-day notice period is the mandatory timeframe a laborer must observe before terminating a labor contract.

Written Notice
Written notice is the formal, documented communication required for a laborer to terminate a labor contract.<SEP>Written Notice is a formal procedural requirement mandating that an Employer provide thirty days’ advance written communication to an Employee prior to contract termination under specified statutory grounds.

Operational Characteristics
Operational characteristics describe the distinctive features of an employer’s production and business activities that influence wage-related decisions.

Article 47
Article 47 is a legal provision stipulating that employers may autonomously determine wage distribution methods and wage levels based on their operational characteristics and economic performance, in accordance with the law.

Wage Distribution Method
The wage distribution method refers to the system or approach used by an employer to allocate wages among employees, determined autonomously in accordance with legal provisions.

Wage Level
The wage level denotes the overall standard or scale of remuneration set by an employer, determined based on operational characteristics, economic performance, and legal requirements.<SEP>Wage level denotes the general standard or amount of wages, which is to be progressively raised in line with economic development.

Economic Performance
Economic performance refers to the financial results and operational efficiency of an employer, serving as a key factor in determining wage distribution and levels.

National Paid Annual Leave System
The National Paid Annual Leave System is a labor policy in China that guarantees paid time off for workers who have been employed continuously for over one year.

Article 45
Article 45 is a provision in Chinese labor law that establishes the national paid annual leave system.

Economic Compensation
Economic compensation is a statutory payment required to be provided by the employer to the employee upon termination of the labor contract under certain legal circumstances.<SEP>Economic compensation is a statutory monetary remedy mandated under Article 91 for certain labor rights violations, distinct from wage restitution or damages.

Article 26
Article 26 is a provision in the Labor Law specifying conditions under which an employer may terminate a labor contract.<SEP>Article 26 is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying conditions under which an employer may lawfully terminate a labor contract with advance written notice.<SEP>Article 26 is a provision in the Labor Contract Law outlining grounds for lawful termination of labor contracts by employers.

Article 24
Article 24 is a provision in the Labor Law specifying conditions under which an employer may terminate a labor contract.<SEP>Article 24 is a provision in Chinese labor law stating that a labor contract may be terminated upon mutual agreement of the contracting parties.

Labor Law
The Labor Law is the primary statutory and national legal framework governing labor relations and employment relationships in China. It serves as the overarching legal instrument that regulates key aspects of the employer-employee relationship, including the formation and termination of labor contracts, statutory requirements for economic compensation upon dismissal or contract expiration, and protections for workers’ fundamental rights.  

The Labor Law establishes binding standards for working conditions, notably setting statutory limits on working hours, mandating rest periods and weekly days off, and defining minimum entitlements to paid annual holidays. Specific provisions—such as Articles 36, 38, and 39—address regular working hours, rest intervals, and exceptions applicable to certain enterprises, while Article 42 outlines additional permissible deviations under defined circumstances. Article 105 situates the Labor Law within the broader hierarchy of Chinese labor legislation, underscoring its foundational role.  

Beyond temporal regulations, the Labor Law also governs occupational safety, wage protections, and the formulation and enforcement of internal labor regulations by employers. It codifies procedural and substantive safeguards for employees, ensuring that contractual and non-contractual labor rights are uniformly upheld across sectors. As the core legislative pillar of China’s labor governance system, the Labor Law integrates substantive rights, procedural obligations, and structural exceptions into a coherent, enforceable framework applicable nationwide.

Article 27
Article 27 is a provision in the Labor Law specifying conditions under which an employer may terminate a labor contract.<SEP>Article 27 is a provision in the Labor Contract Law addressing economic layoff procedures and conditions.

Article 28
Article 28 is a provision in the Labor Law specifying that employers must provide economic compensation when terminating labor contracts under Articles 24, 26, and 27.

Natural Disaster
A natural disaster is an event threatening workers’ life, health, and property safety, triggering exemption from working hour restrictions under Article 42.

Article 41
Article 41 is a legal provision that sets general limits on extended working hours, from which Article 42 provides exemptions.<SEP>Article 41 is a provision from China's Labor Law that regulates the conditions and limits for extending working hours, requiring consultation with the labor union and workers and safeguarding worker health.

Article 42
Article 42 is a legal provision specifying exceptions to restrictions on extended working hours under certain emergency or statutory conditions.

Accident
An accident is an unforeseen event threatening workers’ life, health, and property safety, qualifying for exemption from working hour restrictions under Article 42.

Equipment Failure
Equipment failure refers to malfunctions of production equipment, transportation lines, or public facilities that affect production or public interests and require immediate repair, justifying extended working hours under Article 42.

Public Facilities
Public facilities are infrastructure assets whose failure may impact public interests and necessitate urgent repair under Article 42.

Transportation Lines
Transportation lines are critical infrastructure whose failure affects public interests and triggers the exception for extended working hours under Article 42.

Property Safety
Property safety refers to the protection of workers’ and employers’ property, one of the conditions permitting extended working hours under Article 42.

Labor Safety and Health
Labor safety and health refers to the protection of workers’ life and health, a core concern justifying emergency exceptions to working hour limits in Article 42.<SEP>Labor safety and health is a regulatory domain concerning occupational safety, hygiene standards, and protective measures for workers.

Production Equipment
Production equipment is industrial machinery whose failure affects production and qualifies for exemption from working hour restrictions under Article 42.

Public Interest
Public interest denotes societal welfare concerns affected by infrastructure failures, serving as a basis for extending working hours under Article 42.

Legal and Administrative Regulations
Legal and administrative regulations are statutory instruments that may define additional circumstances allowing extended working hours beyond those listed in Article 42.

Chapter Eight Vocational Training
Chapter Eight Vocational Training is a section or chapter title indicating content related to professional skill development.

Page Thirteen
Page Thirteen is a page number referencing the location of the chapter within a document.

Article Thirty-Four
Article Thirty-Four is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying the procedure for collective contract effectiveness, requiring submission to the labor administration department and establishing the fifteen-day objection period.

Collective Labor Contract
A Collective Labor Contract is a formal agreement negotiated between employers and employee representatives, requiring submission to the labor administration department for validation.

Fifteen-Day Review Period
The Fifteen-Day Review Period is the statutory timeframe during which the Labor Administration Department must review and potentially object to a submitted collective labor contract.

Contract Effectiveness
Contract Effectiveness refers to the legal commencement of a collective labor contract’s binding force, triggered automatically if no objection is raised by the Labor Administration Department within fifteen days.

Labor Administration Department
The Labor Administration Department is the government body responsible for reviewing collective labor contracts and raising objections within fifteen days of receipt.<SEP>The Labor Administration Department is the government agency responsible for enforcing labor laws, including ordering employers to pay overdue social insurance fees.<SEP>The Labor Administration Department is a government body responsible for labor-related administrative functions and oversight.<SEP>The Labor Administration Department is the government body responsible for enforcing labor laws and ordering corrections for violations.<SEP>The Labor Administration Department is the government body responsible for enforcing labor laws and ordering corrections and imposing fines for violations.<SEP>The Labor Administration Department is a government body responsible for supervising labor practices and enforcing labor regulations.

China
China is the country that implements the labor hour system described in the text.

Eight-Hour Workday
The Eight-Hour Workday is a component of the Labor Hour System, limiting daily work time to no more than eight hours.

Forty-Four-Hour Workweek
The Forty-Four-Hour Workweek is a component of the Labor Hour System, limiting average weekly work time to no more than forty-four hours.

Labor Hour System
The Labor Hour System is a regulatory framework specifying maximum daily and weekly working hours for workers.

Chapter Eight
Chapter Eight is a section titled "Vocational Training" in a document or book.

data.entity_name: Chapter Three Labor Contracts and Collective Contracts
data.content:
Chapter Three Labor Contracts and Collective Contracts
Chapter Three Labor Contracts and Collective Contracts is a formal section title in a legal or regulatory document, specifically addressing labor contracts and collective agreements.

Chapter Three
Chapter Three is a section of a legal or regulatory document focusing on labor contracts and collective agreements.<SEP>Chapter Three is a section of a legal or regulatory document titled "Labor Contracts and Collective Contracts".

data.entity_name: Hazard to Life Safety and Health
data.content:
Hazard to Life Safety and Health
Hazard to life safety and health refers to actions or conditions posing direct threats to a worker's physical well-being or survival.

Safety Operating Procedures
Safety operating procedures are formal rules that workers must strictly follow to ensure safety during labor.

Right to Refuse Execution
The right to refuse execution is a legal entitlement allowing workers to decline unlawful or dangerous work orders.

Violation of Command
Violation of command refers to unlawful or unsafe directives issued by employer management personnel.

Forced Risky Work
Forced risky work denotes assignments requiring workers to undertake hazardous tasks against their will or without adequate safety safeguards.

data.entity_name: Right to Criticize, Report, and Accuse
data.content:
Right to Criticize, Report, and Accuse
The right to criticize, report, and accuse enables workers to formally express concerns, file reports, or lodge complaints regarding hazards to life safety and health.

Article 56
Article 56 is a provision in Chinese labor law stipulating workers' rights and obligations regarding workplace safety and management directives.

Probationary Period
The probationary period is a trial phase in a labor contract, with a maximum duration of six months as stipulated by law.

Six-Month Limit
The six-month limit is the statutory maximum duration allowed for a probationary period in a labor contract.

Article 21
Article 21 is a provision in Chinese labor law that regulates the use and maximum duration of probationary periods in labor contracts.

Social Voluntary Labor
Social voluntary labor is an activity promoted by the State for workers to engage in without compensation for societal benefit.

Scientific Research
Scientific research is an activity encouraged and protected by the State for workers to advance knowledge and innovation.

Invention and Creation
Invention and creation involve workers developing new products, methods, or solutions, which the State encourages and protects.

Rationalization Proposal Activity
Rationalization proposal activity involves workers submitting suggestions for improving work processes and efficiency.

Labor Competition
Labor competition is a State-promoted activity aimed at improving productivity and work quality among workers.

Technological Innovation
Technological innovation refers to the development and application of new technologies by workers, supported by the State.

Advanced Worker
An advanced worker is a worker honored and rewarded by the State for exceptional achievements in labor and innovation.

Model Worker
A model worker is a worker recognized and awarded by the State for outstanding contributions and exemplary performance.

Article Six
Article Six is a provision in Chinese labor-related legislation that outlines the State's policies promoting workers' participation in social and innovative activities.

Labor Relationship
A labor relationship is the legally defined connection between a worker and an employer, established via a labor contract.

Article Sixteen
Article Sixteen is a provision in Chinese labor law that mandates the establishment of a labor contract to formalize the labor relationship between a worker and an employer.

Equal Negotiation
Equal negotiation is a legally prescribed method for laborers and employers to discuss and resolve issues concerning labor rights and workplace conditions on a fair and balanced basis.

Workers' Congress
The Workers' Congress is a formal mechanism through which laborers participate in democratic management as stipulated by law.

Democratic Management
Democratic management refers to the participation of laborers in decision-making processes within their workplace through statutory channels such as workers' congresses or assemblies.

Workers' Assembly
The Workers' Assembly is a form of democratic participation used by laborers to manage workplace affairs or negotiate with employers.

Article Eight
Article Eight is a provision in Chinese labor law that stipulates workers' rights to democratic management and equal negotiation with employers for the protection of their lawful rights and interests.

data.entity_name: Legal Rights and Interests of Laborers
data.content:
Legal Rights and Interests of Laborers
The legal rights and interests of laborers encompass protections guaranteed by law, including those related to employment conditions, safety, compensation, and collective representation.

data.entity_name: Conditions for Termination of Labor Contract
data.content:
Conditions for Termination of Labor Contract
Conditions for termination of labor contract is a mandatory clause specifying circumstances under which the contract may lawfully end.

Labor Protection and Working Conditions
Labor protection and working conditions is a mandatory clause covering safety, health, and environmental aspects of employment.

Written Form
Written form is the required mode of execution for labor contracts under Article 19.

Job Content
Job content is a mandatory clause defining the duties and responsibilities of the employee.

Labor Contract Term
Labor contract term is a mandatory clause specifying the duration of the employment relationship.

data.entity_name: Liability for Breach of Labor Contract
data.content:
Liability for Breach of Labor Contract
Liability for breach of labor contract is a mandatory clause defining consequences and responsibilities when either party violates contractual obligations.

Additional Agreed Terms
Additional agreed terms are optional clauses that parties may negotiate and include beyond the mandatory provisions.

Article 19
Article 19 is a provision in Chinese labor law mandating that labor contracts be in written form and include seven specified mandatory clauses.

New Year's Day
New Year's Day is a statutory holiday in China during which employers must grant workers time off.

International Labor Day
International Labor Day, observed on May 1st, is a statutory holiday in China mandating worker rest.

National Day
National Day of the People's Republic of China, celebrated on October 1st, is a statutory holiday requiring employer-provided leave.

Statutory Holiday
A statutory holiday is a legally mandated day off for workers, established by national laws and regulations.<SEP>A statutory holiday is a legally mandated day off, during which employers must pay wages to laborers.

Spring Festival
The Spring Festival is a major traditional Chinese holiday and a statutory holiday requiring employers to arrange worker leave.

Article 40
Article 40 is a provision in China's Labor Law specifying statutory holidays during which employers must arrange worker leave.

Social Insurance Premium
Social insurance premium refers to mandatory contributions made by employers and employees to national social insurance schemes, including pension, medical, unemployment, work-related injury, and maternity insurance.<SEP>Social insurance premiums are mandatory contributions paid by employers and workers to the Social Insurance Fund.

Article One Hundred
Article One Hundred is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying penalties for employers who fail to pay social insurance premiums without justification.

Late Payment Surcharge
A late payment surcharge is an additional fee imposed on employers who fail to pay social insurance premiums within the prescribed time limit.

Labor Administrative Department
The Labor Administrative Department is a government agency operating under the People’s Government at or above the county level, serving as the principal functional authority for labor governance in China. It is legally mandated to supervise and inspect employers’ compliance with labor laws, regulations, and standards—including those governing working hours, rest periods, workplace safety, and occupational health. Among its core enforcement powers, the Labor Administrative Department may issue warnings, order corrective actions, impose administrative fines, and mandate compensation for violations of labor legislation.

In addition to its regulatory and punitive functions, the Labor Administrative Department plays an active role in labor dispute resolution: it is formally represented on the Labor Dispute Arbitration Committee, contributing to oversight and procedural integrity in arbitration proceedings. It also holds statutory authority to approve alternative work and rest arrangements for enterprises—such as flexible scheduling or shift systems—provided these arrangements meet legal requirements and safeguard workers’ rights.

Collectively, the Labor Administrative Department functions as both a preventive supervisor and a responsive enforcer, integrating inspection, education, approval, arbitration participation, and penalty imposition into a comprehensive framework for labor law implementation and worker protection.<SEP>The labor administrative department is the government authority to which employers must report before implementing staff reductions.

Article 38
Article 38 is a statutory provision concerning rest days and holidays, which certain enterprises may be exempted from applying.<SEP>Article 38 is a provision in Chinese labor law mandating that employers guarantee workers at least one day of rest per week.

Article 39
Article 39 is a provision in labor legislation allowing enterprises to adopt alternative work and rest methods under specified conditions.

Wages
Wages refer to the monetary compensation paid to workers for their labor, and is the central topic of Chapter Five.

Chapter Five
Chapter Five is a section titled "Wages" in a document or book.

Chapter Ten
Chapter Ten is titled "Labor Disputes" and outlines procedures for resolving labor-related conflicts.

Chapter Ten Labor Disputes
Chapter Ten outlines procedures and frameworks for handling labor disputes.

Chapter Eleven
Chapter Eleven is titled "Supervision and Inspection" and covers oversight mechanisms in labor law enforcement.

Chapter Thirteen Supplementary Provisions
Chapter Thirteen contains auxiliary rules, transitional clauses, and other supplementary legal provisions.<SEP>Chapter Thirteen Supplementary Provisions is a section title indicating regulatory or legal appendices.

data.entity_name: Chapter Nine Social Insurance and Welfare
data.content:
Chapter Nine Social Insurance and Welfare
Chapter Nine addresses regulations and provisions related to social insurance and welfare systems.

Chapter Nine
Chapter Nine is titled "Social Insurance and Welfare" and addresses related legal provisions.

Chapter Twelve Legal Liability
Chapter Twelve defines legal consequences and responsibilities for violations of labor law.

Chapter Eleven Supervision and Inspection
Chapter Eleven covers mechanisms and responsibilities for supervision and inspection in the context of labor law.

Chapter Twelve
Chapter Twelve is titled "Legal Liability" and specifies consequences for violations of labor regulations.

Chapter Thirteen
Chapter Thirteen is titled "Supplementary Provisions" and contains auxiliary, transitional, or residual legal rules.

Violation-Risk Work
Violation-Risk Work refers to labor activities conducted in breach of safety regulations and involving undue danger to workers.

Major Casualty Accident
A Major Casualty Accident is a serious workplace incident involving significant injury or loss of life resulting from违章冒险作业(violation-risk作业).

Criminal Responsibility
Criminal Responsibility denotes the legal liability that responsible personnel face under criminal law for causing a major casualty accident through unlawful orders.

Serious Consequences
Serious Consequences refer to the severe outcomes, such as loss of life or critical injury, arising from a major casualty accident.

Contract Party
Contract party refers to either the employer or the employee involved in negotiating and entering into a labor contract.

Mutual Agreement
Mutual agreement is the condition under which a labor contract may be terminated, requiring consent from both parties.

Labor Safety and Hygiene Conditions
Labor Safety and Hygiene Conditions refer to workplace standards mandated by national regulations to ensure employee well-being.

Occupational Health Examination
Occupational Health Examination is a periodic medical assessment required for employees engaged in jobs with occupational hazards.

Labor Protective Equipment
Labor Protective Equipment consists of necessary gear provided by employers to safeguard employees from occupational hazards.<SEP>Labor protective equipment includes items provided to workers to safeguard against occupational hazards.

National Regulations
National Regulations refer to the legally binding standards and requirements established by the state governing labor safety, hygiene, protective equipment, and occupational health examinations.<SEP>National regulations are official rules established by the state governing the employment of minors under sixteen in specific sectors.

Article 30
Article 30 is a provision in Chinese labor law that grants labor unions the right to express opinions when employers unilaterally terminate labor contracts.

Labor Union
The Labor Union is an organization that represents employees' interests and has the right to review and provide opinions on employer decisions regarding contract termination.<SEP>The labor union is a representative body that collaborates with the employer and workers regarding working hour extensions.

Law and Regulations
Law and Regulations refer to statutory and administrative rules that govern labor relations and must be adhered to by employers in managing labor contracts.

Ethnic Minority Personnel
Ethnic minority personnel are members of officially recognized ethnic minority groups in China, granted specific employment protections by law.

Demobilized Military Personnel
Demobilized military personnel are individuals who have completed active military service and been discharged, receiving preferential employment treatment under legal provisions.

Law And Regulations
Law and regulations are formal rules established by state authorities that govern employment practices and provide special provisions for designated groups.<SEP>Law and regulations define the conditions and standards under which laborers enjoy social insurance benefits.

Article Fourteen
Article Fourteen is a legal provision specifying employment rules for disabled persons, ethnic minority personnel, and demobilized military personnel.

Disabled Persons
Disabled persons are individuals with physical or mental impairments who are entitled to special employment provisions under law.

Employment
Employment refers to the engagement in lawful work or occupation, subject to special legal provisions for certain protected groups.<SEP>Employment refers to job opportunities created through economic and social development and expanded business activities.<SEP>Employment is a socio-economic concept representing the state of workers being engaged in labor or self-employment.

Savings-Based Insurance
Savings-Based Insurance is a form of personal insurance promoted by the State, emphasizing individual financial planning and security.

Article 75
Article 75 is a provision in Chinese law that outlines state encouragement for employers to establish supplementary insurance and promotion of savings-based insurance for workers.

Supplementary Insurance
Supplementary Insurance is an additional insurance scheme that employers may establish for workers beyond statutory requirements.

Article 46
Article 46 is a provision stipulating principles for wage distribution, including distribution according to labor and equal pay for equal work, as well as state macro-control over total wages.

Equal Pay For Equal Work
Equal pay for equal work is a principle requiring that workers performing the same job with the same responsibilities and qualifications receive identical remuneration.

Distribution According To Labor
Distribution according to labor is a principle stating that wages should be allocated based on the quantity and quality of work performed by individuals.

State Macro-Control Over Total Wages
State macro-control over total wages refers to national-level regulatory measures aimed at managing and guiding the overall scale of wage expenditures in the economy.

Wage Distribution
Wage distribution refers to the system and principles governing how wages are allocated among workers, emphasizing distribution according to labor and equal pay for equal work.

Social Activity
Social activity refers to lawful civic or public engagements in which laborers may participate, entitling them to wage payment during absence from work.

Marriage Leave
Marriage leave is a legally stipulated period of absence granted for marriage, during which wage payment by the employer is required.

Bereavement Leave
Bereavement leave is a legally stipulated period of absence granted for mourning, during which wage payment by the employer is required.

Article 51
Article 51 is a provision in Chinese labor law stipulating that employers must pay wages to laborers during statutory holidays, marriage or bereavement leave, and lawful participation in social activities.

Chapter 6
Chapter 6 is a section of Chinese labor law titled "Labor Safety and Health", establishing regulatory requirements for occupational safety and hygiene.

Employment Services
Employment Services refer to support activities aimed at facilitating job matching and labor market participation, as stipulated in the regulation.

Article 11
Article 11 is a provision mandating local governments to develop vocational introduction institutions and provide employment services.

Local People's Governments
Local People's Governments are governmental bodies at various administrative levels in China responsible for implementing policies including employment service development.

Vocational Introduction Institutions
Vocational Introduction Institutions are entities that provide employment services, and the government is mandated to develop multiple types of them.

Extended Working Hours
Extended working hours refer to additional work time beyond standard hours, permitted only after consultation and subject to health protections.

Compensation Liability
Compensation Liability refers to the legal obligation of the employer to compensate the employee for damages arising from an invalid employment contract.<SEP>Compensation liability refers to the legal obligation of an employer to provide financial redress to a female worker or underage worker harmed due to violation of protective labor provisions.<SEP>Compensation liability refers to the legal obligation of employers to redress harm caused to workers through monetary remedies such as wage restitution, economic compensation, or damages.<SEP>Compensation liability is the legal obligation imposed on a laborer to reimburse the employer for economic losses caused by breach of contract or confidentiality.

Article 97
Article 97 is a legal provision stipulating that employers must bear compensation liability for damages caused to employees by invalid employment contracts attributable to the employer.

Invalid Employment Contract
An Invalid Employment Contract is a contract rendered void due to reasons attributable to the employer, triggering liability for damages.

data.entity_name: Social Insurance Fund Management Institutions Staff
data.content:
Social Insurance Fund Management Institutions Staff
Staff of Social Insurance Fund Management Institutions are personnel responsible for the administration and management of social insurance funds.

Article 104
Article 104 is a legal provision stipulating criminal liability for the misappropriation of social insurance funds by state personnel and staff of social insurance fund management institutions.

State Personnel
State Personnel refers to individuals who hold positions in state organs and are responsible for public duties.

Article 72
Article 72 is a legal provision specifying the funding sources and management principles of the Social Insurance Fund, including mandatory participation by employers and workers.

Social Pooling
Social pooling is a method of managing the Social Insurance Fund whereby funds are collectively pooled and managed at a societal level.

Administrative Region
An administrative region is a geographically defined area under the jurisdiction of a local people's government, where labor work is managed by the corresponding labor administration department.

Labor Work
Labor work refers to the administration, regulation, and oversight of employment, labor relations, and workers' rights in China.

data.entity_name: Local People's Government Labor Administration Department
data.content:
Local People's Government Labor Administration Department
The Local People's Government Labor Administration Department at or above the county level is responsible for managing labor work within its administrative jurisdiction.

State Council Labor Administration Department
The State Council Labor Administration Department is the national authority responsible for overseeing labor work across China.

Article Nine
Article Nine is a provision specifying the administrative responsibilities of labor administration departments at national and local levels in China.

Underage Worker
An underage worker is a worker below the legal working age, entitled to special protections under labor law.

Article 95
Article 95 is a provision in China's Labor Law specifying penalties for employers violating protections for female and underage workers.

Female Worker
A female worker is a worker protected under labor law provisions concerning gender-specific rights and workplace safety.

Labor Safety and Health System
The Labor Safety and Health System is a mandatory institutional framework that employers must establish and maintain to ensure worker safety and health during employment.

data.entity_name: National Labor Safety and Health Regulations and Standards
data.content:
National Labor Safety and Health Regulations and Standards
These are official state-mandated rules and benchmarks governing occupational safety and health practices that employers must strictly follow.

Labor Safety and Health Education
Labor Safety and Health Education is a required activity conducted by employers to inform and train workers on safety and health practices in the workplace.

Article 52
Article 52 is a provision in Chinese labor law mandating employers to establish labor safety and health systems, enforce national regulations and standards, provide safety education, prevent workplace accidents, and reduce occupational hazards.

Occupational Hazard
Occupational Hazard refers to risks or harmful conditions in the work environment that employers must reduce through safety and health measures.

Workplace Accident
A Workplace Accident is an incident occurring during labor processes that employers must prevent through safety measures and education.

Legal Binding Force
Legal binding force refers to the enforceability of a labor contract under law, requiring both parties to fulfill their prescribed obligations.<SEP>Legal binding force refers to the enforceability and validity of a labor contract under law; invalid contracts lack this force from the time of conclusion.

The Law
The Law refers to the legal statute containing Article 107, which comes into effect on January 1, 1995.

January 1 1995
January 1, 1995 is the effective date stipulated for the law's implementation.

Article 107
Article 107 is a provision specifying the effective date of the law.

Job Competency
Job Competency refers to an Employee's ability to fulfill the essential duties of their position, and its deficiency may trigger employer-initiated training or reassignment before potential contract termination.

Medical Leave Period
The Medical Leave Period is a statutorily defined duration during which an Employee is excused from work due to illness or non-work-related injury, after which fitness for work is reassessed.<SEP>The medical leave period is a legally mandated timeframe during which a laborer recovering from illness or injury cannot be dismissed.

Economic Loss
Economic loss refers to financial harm incurred by the original employer as a result of the worker’s breach of contractual obligations.<SEP>Economic loss denotes financial damage incurred by the employer as a result of the laborer’s unlawful actions.

Article 99
Article 99 is a provision in China’s Labor Law stipulating liability for employers hiring workers still under existing labor contracts.

Joint and Several Liability
Joint and several liability is a legal principle requiring the new employer to share responsibility for compensating the original employer’s economic losses.

Original Employer
The original employer is the prior employer of a worker who has not yet terminated their labor contract, and may suffer economic losses due to the worker's new employment.

Article 43
Article 43 is a specific provision in the Labor Law that prohibits employers from extending workers' working hours in violation of legal requirements.

Institutions
Institutions are organizations encouraged by the State to establish industries or expand operations within the bounds of laws and administrative regulations to increase employment.

Enterprises
Enterprises are entities encouraged by the State to establish industries or expand operations within the bounds of laws and administrative regulations to increase employment.

Social Organizations
Social Organizations are groups encouraged by the State to establish industries or expand operations within the bounds of laws and administrative regulations to increase employment.

Economic Development
Economic Development is a key strategy used by the State to foster job creation and expand employment opportunities.

Social Development
Social Development is a key strategy used by the State to foster job creation and expand employment opportunities.

Article Ten
Article Ten is a provision in Chinese law that outlines the State's responsibilities and measures for promoting employment through economic and social development and encouraging various organizations to expand operations legally.

People's Court
The People's Court is the judicial body to which parties may appeal if dissatisfied with an arbitration award in a labor dispute.<SEP>The People's Court is a judicial institution with the authority to confirm the invalidity of labor contracts.

Labor Dispute
A Labor Dispute is a conflict between an employer and a worker that can be resolved through mediation, arbitration, litigation, or negotiation, guided by principles of legality, fairness, and timeliness.

Labor Dispute Arbitration Committee
The Labor Dispute Arbitration Committee handles arbitration requests from parties involved in labor disputes, with members including representatives from labor administrative departments and other relevant entities.<SEP>The Labor Dispute Arbitration Committee is an authorized body empowered to confirm the invalidity of labor contracts.

Labor Dispute Mediation Committee
The Labor Dispute Mediation Committee is established within employers and consists of employee representatives, employer representatives, and trade union representatives; it mediates labor disputes and its chairperson is appointed from the trade union representatives.

Arbitration
Arbitration is a formal legal procedure for resolving labor disputes, initiated either after failed mediation or directly by a party, with decisions subject to judicial review.

Litigation
Litigation is a judicial process available to parties dissatisfied with an arbitration award in a labor dispute, conducted before the People's Court.

Mediation
Mediation is a voluntary, non-binding process applicable to labor disputes, used both independently and within arbitration and litigation procedures.

Individual Business Operations
Individual business operations refer to self-initiated commercial or service activities undertaken by workers to achieve employment.

Regional Economic Development Disparities
Regional Economic Development Disparities denote differences in economic advancement across geographic areas, requiring differentiated minimum wage standards.

Average Wage Level
Average Wage Level represents the mean earnings across all workers in society, used as a reference for setting fair wage floors.

Employment Status
Employment Status reflects the overall condition of the labor market, including job availability and unemployment rates, affecting wage policy decisions.

Living Expenses
Living Expenses refer to the minimum costs required for a worker and their average number of dependents to meet basic subsistence needs.

Article 49
Article 49 is a legal provision specifying the factors to be considered in determining and adjusting the minimum wage standard.

Labor Productivity
Labor Productivity measures the output per unit of labor input and serves as an economic indicator influencing minimum wage adjustments.

Retirement
Retirement is a condition under which a laborer is legally entitled to receive social insurance benefits.
