Injury
Injury is a condition under which a laborer is legally entitled to receive social insurance benefits.

Work-Related Disability
Work-related disability is a condition resulting from a workplace injury or occupational disease, entitling a laborer to social insurance benefits.

Illness
Illness is a condition under which a laborer is legally entitled to receive social insurance benefits.

Occupational Disease
Occupational disease is a health condition caused by work-related hazards, entitling a laborer to social insurance benefits.<SEP>An occupational disease is a health condition caused by work-related hazards and confirmed to result in partial or total loss of working capacity.

Social Insurance Benefits
Social insurance benefits are statutory entitlements provided to laborers in cases such as retirement, illness, injury, work-related disability, occupational disease, unemployment, and maternity.

Unemployment
Unemployment is a condition under which a laborer is legally entitled to receive social insurance benefits.

Maternity
Maternity is a condition under which a laborer is legally entitled to receive social insurance benefits.

Social Insurance Payment
Social insurance payment refers to the mandatory, timely, and full disbursement of social insurance funds to eligible laborers.

Article 73
Article 73 is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying the circumstances under which laborers are entitled to social insurance benefits and related obligations.

Survivor Allowance
Survivor allowance is a statutory benefit paid to the dependents of a deceased laborer.

Dependents
Dependents are family members of a deceased laborer who are legally entitled to survivor allowances.

Chapter Eleven Supervision And Inspection
Chapter Eleven Supervision And Inspection is a section title indicating the topic of supervision and inspection within a document or regulation.

Weekly Rest Day
The weekly rest day is a legally mandated minimum rest period of one day per week for workers.

Other Laws and Administrative Regulations
Other laws and administrative regulations are legal instruments outside the current law that may contain specific penalty clauses for labor rights violations.

Workers' Lawful Rights and Interests
Workers' lawful rights and interests refer to the legally protected entitlements and protections of employees under labor law.

Penalty Provision
The penalty provision specifies that where other laws or administrative regulations already prescribe punishments for violations, those provisions shall apply.

Article 105
Article 105 is a provision in a law that addresses penalties for violations infringing upon workers' lawful rights and interests.

Staff Members
Staff Members are personnel employed by the Labor Administration Department or relevant departments who carry out administrative duties.

Administrative Sanction
Administrative Sanction is a disciplinary measure applied to staff members whose misconduct does not rise to the level of criminal offense.

Favoritism and Malpractice
Favoritism and Malpractice refers to misconduct involving biased or corrupt behavior for personal or others' benefit.

Abuse of Power
Abuse of Power is a form of misconduct where staff members exceed or misuse their authority in violation of regulations.

Relevant Departments
Relevant Departments refer to governmental bodies associated with labor administration and regulation, working alongside or under the Labor Administration Department.<SEP>Relevant Departments are governmental bodies authorized to conduct labor inspections and enforce compliance alongside the Labor Administration Department.

Dereliction of Duty
Dereliction of Duty is a form of misconduct involving negligence or failure to perform official responsibilities.

Article 103
Article 103 is a provision in Chinese labor law specifying legal consequences for misconduct by labor administration staff.

Labor Regulations
Labor regulations are internal rules established by employers that must comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

Article 89
Article 89 specifies administrative penalties-including warnings and corrective orders-for employers whose labor regulations violate laws or regulations, and mandates compensation liability if workers suffer damage.

Article 90
Article 90 prescribes administrative measures-including warnings, corrective orders, and possible fines-against employers who unlawfully extend working hours.

Compensation Payment
Compensation payment refers to the employer’s obligation-enforceable by the Labor Administrative Department-to pay damages to workers harmed by unlawful acts.

Article 91
Article 91 enumerates specific violations such as wage withholding or unjustified delay, and requires the Labor Administrative Department to order payment of wages, economic compensation, and potentially additional damages.

Chapter One General Principles
Chapter One General Principles is the opening chapter of a document or regulation, establishing foundational rules and concepts.

data.entity_name: People's Government at or Above the County Level
data.content:
People's Government at or Above the County Level
The People's Government at or Above the County Level is a governmental body responsible for labor administration and supervision of employers' compliance with labor laws and regulations.<SEP>The People's Government at or Above the County Level is the local administrative authority empowered to supervise labor affairs and direct labor administrative departments.

Labor Law and Regulations
Labor Law and Regulations refer to the statutory framework governing employment relationships, worker rights, and employer obligations in China.

Article 85
Article 85 is a provision in China's Labor Law specifying the supervisory responsibilities of labor administrative departments over employers' compliance with labor laws and regulations.

Labor Employment
Labor employment refers to the provision and facilitation of job opportunities for citizens, promoted by the State through various measures.<SEP>Labor employment refers to the act of workers engaging in paid work, which is protected from discrimination under Article 12.

Vocational Education
Vocational education is a system of skill-based training and education developed by the State to enhance workforce capabilities.

Social Income Regulation
Social income regulation refers to the State's efforts to adjust and balance income distribution across society.

Living Standards of Workers
Living standards of workers refer to the overall quality of life of employed individuals, which the State aims to gradually improve.

Labor Relations Coordination
Labor relations coordination involves the State’s role in mediating and harmonizing interactions between employers and employees.

Labor Standards
Labor standards are regulations established by the State to govern working conditions, rights, and responsibilities in employment relationships.

Article Five
Article Five is a provision in Chinese law that outlines the State's responsibilities regarding labor employment, vocational education, labor standards, income regulation, social insurance, labor relations coordination, and improvement of workers' living standards.

Ethnicity
Ethnicity is a protected characteristic under Article 12, meaning employment decisions must not be influenced by it.

Article 12
Article 12 is a legal provision prohibiting employment discrimination based on ethnicity, race, gender, or religious belief.

Gender
Gender is a protected characteristic under Article 12, meaning employment decisions must not be influenced by it.

Religious Belief
Religious belief is a protected characteristic under Article 12, meaning employment decisions must not be influenced by it.

Race
Race is a protected characteristic under Article 12, meaning employment decisions must not be influenced by it.

Employment Discrimination
Employment discrimination refers to unfair treatment in hiring, promotion, or other employment practices based on prohibited grounds such as ethnicity, race, gender, or religious belief.

Article 57
Article 57 is a provision in Chinese labor legislation that mandates the establishment of statistical reporting and handling systems for workplace accidents and occupational diseases.

data.entity_name: Workplace Accident Reporting and Handling System
data.content:
Workplace Accident Reporting and Handling System
A statutory system established by the State for the statistical reporting and processing of casualties occurring during labor.

data.entity_name: Occupational Disease Reporting and Handling System
data.content:
Occupational Disease Reporting and Handling System
A statutory system established by the State for the statistical reporting and processing of occupational diseases affecting workers.

Socialist Market Economy
Socialist Market Economy is the economic system in the People's Republic of China that the Labor Law aims to support through appropriate labor institutional arrangements.

data.entity_name: Constitution of the People's Republic of China
data.content:
Constitution of the People's Republic of China
The Constitution of the People's Republic of China serves as the foundational legal basis upon which the Labor Law is formulated.

Labor Obligations
Labor Obligations refer to duties workers must fulfill, such as completing assigned tasks, improving vocational skills, complying with occupational safety and health regulations, and observing labor discipline and professional ethics.

Labor Rights
Labor Rights encompass statutory entitlements of workers, including equal employment, fair remuneration, rest and vacation, occupational safety and health, vocational training, social insurance, and labor dispute resolution.

Employer Rules and Regulations
Employer Rules and Regulations are internal systems that employers must legally establish and improve to safeguard workers' rights and ensure fulfillment of labor obligations.

Article 1
Article 1 of the Labor Law of the People's Republic of China states the law's purpose: to protect workers' rights, adjust labor relations, establish and maintain a labor system adapted to the socialist market economy, and promote economic development and social progress, based on the Constitution.

Article 2
Article 2 of the Labor Law of the People's Republic of China defines its scope of application, covering enterprises and individual economic organizations (collectively termed employers) and their workers within the People's Republic of China, as well as state organs, public institutions, and social organizations that enter into labor contracts with workers.

Article 4
Article 4 of the Labor Law of the People's Republic of China mandates that employers must lawfully formulate and improve internal rules and regulations to guarantee workers' rights and ensure fulfillment of labor obligations.

Article 3
Article 3 of the Labor Law of the People's Republic of China enumerates workers' rights-including equal employment, remuneration, rest and vacation, occupational safety and health protection, vocational training, social insurance and welfare, and labor dispute resolution-as well as their obligations, such as completing work tasks, improving skills, complying with safety regulations, and observing labor discipline and professional ethics.

Unauthorized Termination of Labor Contract
Unauthorized termination refers to a laborer ending a labor contract in violation of legally prescribed conditions.

Breach of Confidentiality
Breach of confidentiality refers to the violation of agreed-upon confidentiality terms in a labor contract, potentially causing economic loss to the employer.

Article 102
Article 102 is a specific provision in the Labor Contract Law that stipulates compensation liability for laborers who unlawfully terminate contracts or breach confidentiality obligations.

People's Republic of China
The People's Republic of China is the sovereign state whose national law governs the Labor Contract System and authorizes provincial governments to implement it.

Article 106
Article 106 is a provision specifying the authority of provincial-level governments to define implementation steps for the labor contract system and requiring reporting to the State Council.

data.entity_name: Provincial, Autonomous Region, and Municipality Directly Under the Central Government
data.content:
Provincial, Autonomous Region, and Municipality Directly Under the Central Government
These are first-level administrative divisions of the People's Republic of China where the Labor Contract System implementation steps are locally prescribed.

Labor Contract System
The Labor Contract System is a legal framework governing employment relationships, established by national law and implemented locally with specified procedural steps.

data.entity_name: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
data.content:
Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
The Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China is the statutory legal framework governing labor contracts, under which Article 22 is enacted.<SEP>The Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China is the comprehensive statutory framework governing employment relationships, including protections against unjust termination as specified in Article 29.

Article 22
Article 22 is a provision in Chinese labor law that permits inclusion of confidentiality clauses in labor contracts.

Commercial Secret
A Commercial Secret refers to confidential business information protected under labor contract provisions to maintain competitive advantage.

Article Thirteen
Article Thirteen is a legal provision guaranteeing women equal employment rights with men, except for state-designated occupations unsuitable for women.

Women
Women are a demographic group granted equal employment rights with men under the specified regulation.

State-Designated Occupations
State-designated occupations are specific jobs or positions officially determined by the state as unsuitable for women, forming an exception to gender-based employment equality.

Employment Rights
Employment rights refer to the legal entitlements related to hiring, job assignment, and workplace treatment, guaranteed equally to women and men.

Men
Men are a demographic group with whom women share equal employment rights under the specified regulation.

County-Level or Higher People's Government
The County-Level or Higher People's Government has the authority to order production suspension and rectification in cases of serious labor safety violations.

Labor Safety Facilities
Labor safety facilities are physical installations required by national standards to ensure workplace safety.

Labor Hygiene Conditions
Labor hygiene conditions refer to environmental and health-related standards in the workplace mandated by national regulations.

Major Accident
A major accident is a workplace incident resulting from unaddressed hazards, causing significant loss of life or property.

Article 92
Article 92 is a provision in China's labor law specifying legal consequences for employers failing to meet labor safety and hygiene requirements.

Article 18
Article 18 is a legal provision specifying conditions under which labor contracts are deemed invalid.

Fraud or Coercion
Fraud or coercion denotes improper means such as deception or threats used in concluding a labor contract, which invalidates it per Article 18.

Law Violation
Law violation refers to the breach of laws or administrative regulations in the formation of a labor contract, rendering it invalid under Article 18.

Invalid Labor Contract
An invalid labor contract is one that violates laws or administrative regulations, or is concluded through fraud, coercion, or other improper means, and thus lacks legal binding force from inception.

Article 86
Article 86 is a provision in Chinese labor law that stipulates the supervisory and inspection duties of labor administrative departments at or above the county level.

Article 32
Article 32 is a provision in China's Labor Law that enumerates specific circumstances under which a laborer may terminate a labor contract immediately by notifying the employer.

Working Conditions
Working conditions refer to the environment, safety, tools, and other prerequisites provided by the employer as agreed upon in the labor contract.

Article 98
Article 98 is a provision in China's Labor Law specifying employer liabilities for unlawful termination or delay in concluding employment contracts and the administrative remedies available.

Employment Contract
The employment contract is a legally binding agreement between employer and employee, central to the provisions of this article.

Law and Administrative Regulations
These are binding legal provisions established by state legislative and administrative authorities that labor contracts must not violate.

Article Seventeen
Article Seventeen is a provision in Chinese labor law that stipulates principles for establishing and amending labor contracts.

Principle of Consensus Through Consultation
This principle mandates that any establishment or amendment of a labor contract must result from mutual agreement reached through discussion.

Principle of Equality and Voluntariness
This principle requires that both parties to a labor contract enter into or modify the agreement freely and on equal footing.

Special Craft Units
Special craft units are organizations practicing traditional or specialized crafts and may employ minors under sixteen subject to national regulatory requirements.

Minor Under Sixteen
A minor under sixteen refers to a person younger than sixteen years old, whose employment is generally prohibited but allowed in certain sectors under national regulations.<SEP>A minor under sixteen refers to a person below the age of sixteen, whose employment is prohibited by labor law.

Cultural Institutions
Cultural institutions are organizations involved in arts and entertainment and may employ minors under sixteen in accordance with national regulations.

Sports Organizations
Sports organizations are entities engaged in athletic training and competition and may employ minors under sixteen in compliance with national regulations.

Compulsory Education
Compulsory education is a legally mandated educational requirement that must be safeguarded for minors even when employed.

Article Fifteen
Article Fifteen is a legal provision prohibiting employers from hiring minors under sixteen years of age, with exceptions for cultural, sports, and special craft units subject to national regulations and compulsory education safeguards.

data.entity_name: People's Republic of China Labor Law
data.content:
People's Republic of China Labor Law
The People's Republic of China Labor Law is a statutory legal document enacted by the National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China to regulate labor relations and protect the rights and interests of workers.

Statutory Restructuring
Statutory restructuring refers to the legally mandated reorganization process undertaken by employers facing imminent bankruptcy, serving as a condition for lawful staff reduction.

Staff Reduction
Staff reduction is a legally regulated personnel adjustment measure permitted only under conditions of statutory restructuring or serious operational hardship.

Priority Re-Employment
Priority re-employment is the legal obligation of an employer to give preferential hiring consideration to previously laid-off employees within six months of their termination.

Employees
Employees are the workers who may be subject to reduction and must be informed and consulted during the process; they also hold priority re-employment rights within six months.

Severe Operational Hardship
Severe operational hardship denotes a critical deterioration in an employer's business conditions, constituting another legally recognized basis for staff reduction.

Article Seven
Article Seven is a legal provision granting laborers the right to join and organize trade unions and affirming the independent operation of trade unions.

Market Regulation Department
The Market Regulation Department is the government body authorized to revoke business licenses in cases of serious labor law violations.

Article 94
Article 94 is a provision in Chinese labor legislation specifying penalties for illegal employment of minors under sixteen.

Business License
A business license is an official document permitting an organization to operate legally, which may be revoked for severe violations.

Maternity Leave
Maternity leave is a legally guaranteed period following childbirth during which female laborers are protected from contract termination.

Work-Related Injury
A work-related injury is a physical harm sustained during employment and confirmed to result in partial or total loss of working capacity.

Article 29
Article 29 is a provision in the Labor Contract Law specifying circumstances under which employers may not terminate labor contracts.

Nursing Period
The nursing period refers to the time after maternity leave when a female laborer is breastfeeding and remains protected from dismissal.

Chapter Seven
Chapter Seven is a section of a legal or regulatory document focusing on special protections for female workers and underage workers.

Special Protection
Special protection denotes legally mandated safeguards for vulnerable worker groups such as female and underage workers.

data.entity_name: Chapter Seven Female Workers and Underage Workers Special Protection
data.content:
Chapter Seven Female Workers and Underage Workers Special Protection
Chapter Seven is titled 'Female Workers and Underage Workers Special Protection' and constitutes a formal section of labor legislation.

Underage Workers
Underage workers refer to individuals below the legal working age who are granted special protections under labor regulations.

Chapter Two
Chapter Two is titled "Promoting Employment" and focuses on employment-related policies or initiatives.

Employment Promotion
Employment Promotion refers to strategies, measures, or policies aimed at increasing job opportunities and reducing unemployment.

Article 101
Article 101 is a legal provision stipulating penalties for employers who obstruct labor inspections or retaliate against whistleblowers.

Whistleblower
The Whistleblower is an individual who reports violations of labor law and is protected from retaliation under this provision.
